name: How to Add JSON-LD Structured Data to a Next.js Blog Post
description: Add Article and FAQPage JSON-LD to a Next.js 15 blog route so that Google AI Overviews and Perplexity can cite the page. Tested on Next.js 15.2 with the App Router.

supply.name: Existing Next.js 15 project with App Router

step.name: Create the JSON-LD generator helper
step.text: Add lib/jsonld.ts that exports buildArticleJsonLd(post) returning an Article object with @context, @type, headline, datePublished, dateModified, author, and publisher. Pull values from frontmatter; never hard-code titles.

step.name: Inject the script tag in the route layout
step.text: In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx, render <script type="application/ld+json" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonld) }} />. Place it in the <head> via the metadata API or directly in the component tree.

step.name: Add FAQPage schema for any Q&A block
step.text: If the post contains a FAQ section, build a parallel FAQPage object with mainEntity as an array of Question/Answer pairs. Question text must literally match the H3 in the prose so retrievers de-duplicate cleanly.

step.name: Validate with Google Rich Results Test
step.text: Deploy to a preview URL and paste it into search.google.com/test/rich-results. Confirm zero errors and zero warnings on Article and FAQPage. Fix any missing required fields (image, author.name, datePublished) before promoting to production.

step.name: Verify ingestion in Search Console
step.text: Within 7-14 days, check Search Console > Enhancements > Article and FAQ reports for valid items. If counts stay at zero, re-validate the URL with the URL Inspection tool and request indexing.
